title: A People's History of the United States
subtitle: 1492 — Present
author: Howard Zinn
themes: History from the bottom up — the perspective of the powerless
Class struggle as the engine of American history
The manipulation of nationalism and patriotism by elites
Racism as a deliberate tool of economic control
Resistance movements as the source of genuine progress
War as an instrument of elite power and imperial expansion
The gap between American ideals and American reality
awards: Prix des Amis du Monde Diplomatique (2003, French edition)
excerpt: History from the bottom up. Howard Zinn's classic shifts the lens from presidents and generals to the workers, women, Indigenous peoples, and enslaved who built — and resisted — the United States.
